text: Planning a trip is the first step of any great adventure. When I visited Japan last year, I spent weeks researching places to stay and must-see sights. Experiencing new cultures was incredible, from ancient temples in Kyoto to the neon lights of Tokyo. The food was a highlight—trying fresh sushi and savory ramen was unforgettable. However, language barriers were tricky; I often used hand gestures and translation apps to order meals or ask for directions. Despite these challenges, the memories I made—like sharing a meal with a local family or getting lost in a peaceful garden—are priceless. Every journey teaches you something about the world and yourself.
